pr.title: Splitting of some debug code and lbsnap out from larger text sections
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Split several large monolithic assembly text sections into more specific subsystem assembly files, including debug code, lbsnap, mndiagram, and large item ranges, then added the new objects to obj_files.mk in link-order positions. The PR appears to be an organizational/decomp-enabling assembly split rather than a semantic code change; evidence is the near-balanced added/deleted line counts and the moved .section address ranges.

key_files.role: new debug-region assembly split from text_3.s
key_files.evidence: New file with .section .text range 0x80225374 - 0x802295A8 and 4809 added lines.
Section range changed from 0x80225374 - 0x80233A94 to 0x802295AC - 0x80233A94, with 4807 deleted lines.
New file with .section .text range 0x8001D338 - 0x8001E2F4 and 1119 added lines.
New file with .section .text range 0x8001E2F8 - 0x80028B98 and 12144 added lines.
New file added with 9962 lines; mndiagram.o added after mn/code_8023B0F8.o in obj_files.mk.

key_files.role: original menu section shortened before mndiagram
new item-region assembly split from text_4.s
key_files.evidence: New file with .section .text range 0x80266F3C - 0x8027CF2C and 25619 added lines.

key_files.role: new large item-region assembly split from text_4.s
key_files.evidence: New file with .section .text range 0x8027CF30 - 0x802F3390 and 134587 added lines.
Section range reduced to 0x802F3394 - 0x8032261C after deleting 160201 lines.

key_files.role: build object list updated for split files
key_files.evidence: Added lbsnap.o, code_8001E2F8.o, db/code_80225374.o, mndiagram.o, it/code_80266F3C.o, and it/code_8027CF30.o.

what_changed: Moved the address range 0x80225374 - 0x802295A8 out of asm/melee/text_3.s into asm/melee/db/code_80225374.s.
Shortened asm/melee/text_3.s so its .text section now starts at 0x802295AC.

Split asm/melee/lb/code_80016A54.s into an earlier range ending at 0x8001D334, a named lbsnap.s range from 0x8001D338 - 0x8001E2F4, and a continuation code_8001E2F8.s range from 0x8001E2F8 - 0x80028B98.
Split asm/melee/mn/code_8023B0F8.s so the earlier file ends at 0x8023EA28 and a new mndiagram.s file carries the following menu code through 0x802474C0.
Moved two large ranges out of asm/melee/text_4.s into asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s and asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s.
Reduced asm/melee/text_4.s to the remaining range 0x802F3394 - 0x8032261C.
Registered the new assembly objects in obj_files.mk at positions consistent with their original address/link order.
smart_moves: Preserved contiguous address-range boundaries in .section comments, making the split auditable against the original monolithic text sections.

Kept unknown chunks named by their starting address, such as code_80225374.s, code_80266F3C.s, and code_8027CF30.s, while using more meaningful subsystem names where known, such as lbsnap.s and mndiagram.s.
Updated obj_files.mk alongside the file moves, which is essential for split assembly to link in the same order as the original text section.
Performed a mostly mechanical relocation with balanced additions and deletions, reducing review surface to boundaries and build ordering rather than rewriting assembly.
decomp_lessons: When breaking up giant asm text_N.s files, split on exact address boundaries and update the remaining file's .section range to document the new ownership.
Add each split assembly file to TEXT_O_FILES immediately where its original address range belongs; object ordering is part of matching.
Use subsystem directories once a range is identified, even if individual functions remain unnamed, because this improves future search and ownership for decomp work.
A PR that only relocates assembly can still be valuable: smaller asm files make future C conversion, symbol naming, and subsystem review more tractable.
naming_conventions: Unknown assembly files use code_<start address>.s with uppercase hex in the filename, e.g. code_80266F3C.s and code_8001E2F8.s.
Known or inferred modules can use descriptive lowercase names in their subsystem directory, e.g. asm/melee/lb/lbsnap.s and asm/melee/mn/mndiagram.s.
Assembly labels and globals remain address-derived when not yet decompiled or named, e.g. func_80225374 and lbl_80225388.
Subsystem directory placement follows Melee prefixes: db for debug, lb for library/support code, mn for menu, and it for item.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserve .include "macros.inc" at the top of each new assembly file.
Preserve .global function labels and address-commented instruction lines during file relocation.
Use .section .text comments with exact start/end ranges to track split boundaries.
Maintain link order in obj_files.mk so newly split objects replace the removed ranges without shifting the final binary layout.
Leave the original text_3.s and text_4.s files as shortened remainder sections rather than deleting them outright.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided context, so no human reviewer rationale or requested changes can be extracted.

searchable_terms: Splitting of some debug code and lbsnap out from larger text sections
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or rename functions from asm/melee/db/code_80225374.s.
Find later PRs touching asm/melee/lb/lbsnap.s or converting lbsnap assembly to C.
Find later PRs touching asm/melee/mn/mndiagram.s for menu diagram decompilation.
Find later PRs that further split asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s or asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s.
Check whether the mndiagram.s .section comment typo-like range prefix '08023EA2C' was later corrected to '0x8023EA2C'.
